metadata.title:
LOOKS BAD


TO GREENE

MEMBER OF COMMISSION SAYS
IT MUST BE IMPROVED OR


HE WILL FIGHT

CAN'T IGNORE PEOPLE

pages.raw_text:
Ex-County Auditor r. p. Greene,
member Of the charter commits.
sion, said this morning that there
was a number of features Of the
proposed city charter which he
would oppose. He said that unless
the present defects In the charter
were remedied he would fight the
charter before the people.


THE people should at all times
have control of the officials in order
to make the commission form of
government "a success. The right
of recall and the initiative and ref
erendum should surely be in such
shape that the voters could make
use of them. Without these !
would be opposed to commission
form of government 1t would
make possible the building up In
this city political machine which
would be worse than Tammany.


"The proposition to hold annual
elections should be beaten" con-
tinued Mr.. Greene. THE city can
not afford to hold annual elections.
They cost too much money to be
held annually and the voters will
never stand for such an innovation,
in my judgment.


UNLESS the other members of
the charter committee can be
brought. to. realize that the pro-
posed charter is likely to be unsat-
isfactory to the public and needed
changes made l will feel 1t my
duty to the public to oppose the
entire proposition. would rather
continue under the present charter
than to adopt one such as now pro-
posed by the committee"
